briefinfo: An academic and research leader with over 20 years of distinguished service across premier institutions, including IIT Jammu, IIT Hyderabad, and KU Leuven (QS World Rank #42) and Industry. Adept at institution building from the ground up, policy development, strategic planning, and academic governance, with transformative roles as Dean, National Coordinator, and Head of Section. Authored over 100 scientific publications in Journals of high impact factor and conferences of international repute and led multi-crore research and infrastructure projects. An advocate and practitioner for NEP 2020, interdisciplinary innovation, and global academic collaboration
researchinterest: Manufacturing, Additive Manufacturing, Material Joining

briefinfo:
I am a condensed matter theorist interested in exploring dynamical and transport properties of systems away or close to quantum critical point of certain types. I joined IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor in March, 2025. I look forward to doing exciting science in collaboration with enthusiastic students and postdocs.

 

Recent news from the group:

* Our new work on resonant Edelstein and inverse Edelstein effects, charge-to-spin conversion, and spin pumping from chiral spin modes has been published in Physical Review B (Perspective) [DOI: https://doi.org/10.1103/5p54-96d7] as a contribution to a special issue dedicated to the memory of late Emmanuel Rashba.

* Our recent work on a tight-binding and density-functional investigation of the Raman tensor in 2D massive Dirac fermion systems has been published in Physical Review B [https://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/m1p1-w3w4].
researchinterest:
Quantum polar phase transition

Transport in correlated electron systems

Fermi-liquid with spin-orbit coupling

Theory of Raman, electron spin resonance (ESR) and electric-dipole spin resonance (EDSR) spectroscopies

Strongly correlated systems: cuprates high temperature superconductors, flat-band systems

2) Abhishek Kumar, V. I. Yudson, and D. L. Maslov, “Quasiparticle and nonquasiparticle transport in doped quantum paraelectrics,” Phys. Rev. Lett., vol. 126, p. 076601 (2021).

briefinfo: Dr. Ajay Gautam is an Assistant Professor in Materials Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research focuses on the structure–transport relationships in materials for lithium-ion and solid-state batteries, advanced materials processing, and characterization using X-ray and neutron diffraction. Previously, he was an R&D Scientist at Samsung SDI Europe GmbH, Germany, where he focused on the processing of battery materials for next-generation battery technologies. Dr. Gautam earned his Ph.D. at the University of Münster and Justus Liebig University Giessen, Germany, under the supervision of Prof. Wolfgang Zeier. His doctoral work established key parameters of structural disorder and ion kinetics to unravel the structure–ion transport correlation in solid electrolytes. He pursued postdoctoral research with Prof. Marnix Wagemaker at Technical University of Delft, the Netherlands. There, he worked on the development of solid electrolytes, investigated their structure–transport properties using neutron diffraction, and explored silicon anode–solid electrolyte systems with a focus on degradation mechanisms and scaling strategies toward industrial relevance. He completed his B.Sc. (Research) and M.Sc. in Materials Science at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Bangalore, where he explored carbon nanotubes with transition metals for electrochemical and gas sensing applications.

briefinfo:
Dr. Ambika Prasad Shah is currently working as an Associate Professor in Electrical Engineering Department at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, India. He received Ph.D. degree from the Electrical Engineering Department, Indian Institute of Technology Indore, India. Before joining IIT Jammu, Dr. Shah worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at the Institute for Microelectronics, TU Vienna, Austria.

He is the recipient of the Young Scientist Award from the M.P. Council of Science and Technology Bhopal, M.P. India. He received a Young Faculty Research Fellowship (YFRF) from the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India in 2025. He was nominated for the 10th BRICS Young Scientist Forum 2025 in Brazil by DST, Govt. of India. He is the recipient of the Best Paper Award for two consecutive years at the VDAT-2024 and VDAT-2025, Second Best Award at the 7th International Conference on Microelectronics, Circuits and Systems (Micro-2020), and was nominated for the Best Paper from 31st IEEE Conference on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI-SoC 2023).

He has authored/co-authored more than 110 research papers in peer-reviewed international journals and conferences and authored two books. He was the Conference Organizing Chair for VDAT-2022, Fellowship Chair for VLSID-2022, Publicity Chair for iSES 2023, and Fellowship Chair for VDAT 2025. He coordinates the VLSI Society of India, Himalayan Region Chapter. He is the associate editor of IEEE Transactions on VLSI Systems and IEEE Transactions on Dependable and Secure Computing, editor of Nature Scientific Reports and IETE Journal of Research, and guest editor of Springer and Elsevier journals. He is a fellow of IETE, a senior member of IEEE, and a member of ACM, ISTE, and IEI.

His current research interest includes reliability analysis of digital circuits, circuits and systems for biomedical applications, fault-tolerant circuits, low-power high-performance circuit designs, and Hardware security primitives.

briefinfo:
Dr. Amrita Chatterjee is currently employed as an Assistant Professor in Chemical Engineering department at IIT Jammu. She is working on green energy technologies, with research spanning over batteries, supercapacitors, photo/electrocatalysis, and biomass valorization. Her work focuses on designing functional porous materials like MOF, COF, and hierarchical carbon and engineering these next generation materials to device-level solutions for sustainable energy and chemical conversion.

Kindly email at amrita.chatterjee@iitjammu.ac.in for any possible collaborations or interest.
researchinterest:
1) Pore engineering in metal and covalent organic frameworks ( MOFs and COFs) and hierarchical carbon.
2) Electrode design and synthesis for lithium ion and sodium ion batteries and supercapacitors.
3) Electrolyte synthesis and interface engineering in solid state batteries for room temperature and sub zero applications.
4) Photo and electrocatalysis for waste- to- energy via CO2 reduction, water splitting, organic transformation, quantifying intrinsic kinetics versus mass transport.

briefinfo: Currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry and the Interdisciplinary Program (IDP) at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, Dr. Anil Kumar Paidi obtained his Ph.D. in Chemistry from IIT Madras, India, in July 2017. Following his doctoral studies, he pursued postdoctoral research in the Department of MEMS at IIT Bombay, focusing on the development of novel cathode materials. Subsequently, he worked as a Research Scientist at the Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PAL), associated with POSTECH, South Korea, where he delivered outstanding research contributions in sustainable battery materials.

otherinfo:
Biography:

Ankit received the B.E. degree in Electronics and Telecommunication Engineering from the Chhattisgarh Swami Vivekanand Technical University (CSVTU), Bhilai-India in 2009 and the Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Delhi-India in 2014. In 2012 and 2013, he was a visiting research scholar at the University of British Columbia, Vancouver, Canada. From July 2014 to December 2014 he has been a research associate with IIT Delhi. From December 2014 to Dec 2018, he has been with the faculty of the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering, National Institute of Technology (NIT), Goa-India, where he was an Assistant Professor. Since January 2019, he has been with the faculty of the Department of Electrical Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT), Jammu-India, where he is currently an Assistant Professor and Head of the Department. He has published several journal and conference papers in the area of communications engineering. He has several govt. of India sponsored projects worth 15 Millions INR.  He is also working on multidisciplinary research project on indigenous UAV systems funded by IIT Jammu with fund up to the tune of 40 Million INR. His research interests are in diversity combining, multi-hop transmission, and physical layer security, PLC, IoT, and wireless communications. He works in the application side for smart grid communications, automation, IoT, UAV detection using radar, etc.

briefinfo:
Get to know the iMaSS lab, click the link for details: https://sites.google.com/view/imass

About me

Dr. Ankit Kathuria is working as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering and Humanities and Social Sciences Department at IIT Jammu. Throughout his career, he has undertaken diverse roles, including his current position as Associate Dean for E-Governance System and his previous role as Head of the Department of Civil Engineering and Associate Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). He received the prestigious SERB-SIRE Fellowship in 2022, allowing him to contribute to significant research related to roundabout safety at the Future Mobility Group, Newcastle University, UK, for six months.

Dr. Kathuria did postdoctoral research at the Indian Institute of Technology in Bombay, India. He obtained his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering (Transportation Engineering) from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee in 2017. He received a Masters in Planning degree with specialization in Transport Planning from the School of Planning and Architecture, New Delhi in 2013 and, holds a Bachelor of Technology degree in Civil Engineering from Jaypee University, India.

In the realm of research, Dr. Kathuria has led various projects funded by esteemed organizations such as the Ministry of Road Transportation and Highways, Science and Engineering Research Board, National Highway Authority of India, Central Road Research Institute, Jammu Kashmir Science Technology and Innovation Council, and Indian Institute of Technology Jammu.

Dr. Kathuria's focus on research is driven by a genuine desire to find practical solutions to various transportation planning and engineering problems. His research encompasses a range of disciplines, including behavioral and psychological aspects of traffic, road safety, public transportation system design and planning, traffic management using BT and wifi sensors, social sciences, data mining, mathematical modeling, and flexible pavements (secondary research area). Presently, he leads six research projects as the principal investigator, aligning with his research interests.

In his mentoring capacity, Dr. Kathuria guides seven Ph.D. students, out of which one student has already submitted his thesis for further evaluation. He is grateful for the opportunities to contribute to academic discourse, with over 47 peer-reviewed research and conference papers to his name. Additionally, he is honored to serve as a reviewer for respected journals such as TR part A, TR part D, Accident Analysis and Prevention, Case Study on Transport Policy, Transport Policy, Transportation Letters and Journal of Transportation Engineering. Dr. Kathuria remains deeply committed to advancing knowledge and expertise in the humble pursuit of contributing to the field of transportation Planning and engineering.

a) Behavioural and psychological aspects of drivers in heterogeneous disordered traffic conditions

b) Proactive approaches to road safety, including the use of surrogate safety measures

d) Simulation-based AV's and human-driven vehicle interaction

e) Public transportation system design and planning with primary focus on Bus Rapid Transit Systems

f) Exploring adoption intentions of e-shared mobility alternatives

g) Traffic management using BT and Wifi sensors

Secondary research interest

h) Utilization of surface-modified aggregates in asphalt mixes

briefinfo:
I am working as Professor in Civil Engineering Department and Dean (Infrastructure Planning and Management). Before joining IIT Jammu, I worked as Chief Executive Officer of an infrastructure company where I was taking care of planning, designing and execution of Pre-engineered steel warehousing complexes and steel-concrete composite elevated road project. I also played a pivotal role in expanding the company and establishment of an innovative automated manufacturing unit, which is a state-of-the-art facility for manufacturing precast sections and heavy prefab steel structures for public infrastructure sector & prefab multi storey buildings.

Before taking up this industrial assignment, I worked in academia as Professor & Head of Structural Engineering, Coordinator of Centre for Building Technology and Construction Materials at Malaviya National Institute of Technology, Jaipur and Director of Anand International College of Engineering, Jaipur. I also worked at Liverpool John Moores University, Liverpool (U.K.) and Curtins Consulting Engineers (U.K.) under U.K. India REC’s Project for six months in the area of Structural Design and Construction.

My research focuses on Utilization of Wastes in Concrete, Durability of Concrete, Concrete Repairs and Non-destructive Testing of Concrete. I have been involved in supervising projects in the area of Durability of High-Volume Fly ash Concrete, Self-compacting Concrete, Use of Wollastonite in Concrete and Durability of Concrete Containing ISF Slag, Geopolymer Concrete, Use of Jarosite in Concrete, etc. I have also been associated with collaborative research project "High Performance Sustainable Concrete Infrastructure" jointly with University of Dundee (U.K.) and eight Indian Institutions, funded by UK India Education and research Initiative (UKIERI). I also executed more than 200 consultancy projects including Jaipur Metro Project.

Presently I am looking for motivated Post-Doctoral/ PhD/ Internship candidates who would be interested in working on development of low carbon cement, Non-destructive Testing with IoT applications and Prestressed Composite bridges, High Strength Steel welding. Interested persons may send an email: anurag.misra@iitjammu.ac.in

researchinterest: My research work focuses on first-principles quantum theory of advanced functional materials. It not only aims at achieving microscopic insights into exotic states of fundamentally interesting materials (frustrated magnets, multiferroics, superconductors, Hund’s metals and topological superconductors), but also designing materials of technological importance (multiferroics, superconductors and transparent conductors).

briefinfo: Dr. Verma earned his master’s and Ph.D. from I.I.T. Delhi, New Delhi, India. He served as a visiting professor at Ontario Tech University, Oshawa, Ontario, Canada, in 2024. He was a Postdoctoral research fellow (PDF) at the Energy Research Institute (ERI@N), Nanyang Technological University (NTU) Singapore, from 2015 to 2016. Before joining NTU Singapore, he worked as a visiting graduate researcher (VGR) at the Smart Grid Energy Research Center (SMERC), University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA), California, USA, from 2014 to 2015. He won the prestigious BASE fellowship for advanced solar energy research in 2014. He has been conferred with the prestigious POSOCO Power System Research Award 2016. He was also selected for the SERB International Research Experience (SIRE) award in 2023. He is an associate Editor for IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification and an associate editor for IEEE Journal of Emerging and Selected Topics in Industrial Electronics, as well as an assistant editor of the Smart Grid journal and a member of the editorial board for the EPE Journal. He has been invited by Defence University College, Ethiopia, and Metal Engineering Corporation, Ethiopia, as an instructor for the M.Tech program in Renewable Energy Engineering from 7 May 2016 to 27 May 2016. To name a few. He has/completed DST research funding of 7.5 cr., including Mission Innovation (MI), SPARC, ECR, SERD, and CRG. He is also the mentor director of a startup, Rishi Agastaya Technologies Pvt. Ltd. Dr Verma has guided 10 PhD and 18 M.Tech theses. Six PhD candidates are currently working under his supervision.

briefinfo: Dr. Arvind Kumar Rajput is a faculty member in the Department of Mechanical Engineering specializing in Tribology, Fluid Film Bearings, and Advanced Lubrication Systems. His research focuses on hydrodynamic and hybrid journal bearings, porous and gas-lubricated bearings  functioing with advanced lubricants.  Dr. Rajput teaches Tribology, Solid Mechanics and Machine Design, and is a recipient of the Teaching Appreciation Award for his academic contributions for the academic year 2019-21. He also serves as a Board of Studies (BOS) Member at Government College of Engineering and Technology Jammu.

briefinfo:
Ashutosh Bijalwan is an Assistant Professor in Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. His research spans Applied Mechanics, with emphasis on Optimal Control, Computational Mechanics, and Numerical Methods, particularly applied to soft and living systems.

He earned his PhD in Applied Mathematics with Excellent Cum Laude from CIMNE–UPC, Barcelona, Spain, where he worked on structure-preserving numerical strategies for optimal control of mechanical systems. He completed his Master’s in Engineering Mechanics from the Applied Mechanics Department at IIT Delhi, where he received the Prof. B. Karunesh Memorial Award for his thesis on visco-hyperelastic analysis of the ACL.

He previously worked at Eaton and Schlumberger as a Modeling and Simulation Engineer and later as a Research Engineer (Wells) in the Geomechanics Group at ExxonMobil, where he conducted research in multiscale modeling, uncertainty quantification, and inverse problems related to the mechanics of hydraulic fracturing in unconventional reservoirs.

His current research explores structure-preserving and multiscale computational frameworks for soft and living systems, with applications in tissue morphogenesis, biomedical devices, and bioinspired soft robotics.

briefinfo: Dr. Deepak Yadav is presently working as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu. He did PhD in Structural Engineering from IIT Delhi and BTech in Civil Engineering from IIT Roorkee. His doctoral research was focused on metallic-yielding dampers and he filed a patent on the developed damper as co-inventor. His research interests are seismic response control, retrofitting of structures, steel and aluminum structures, and earthquake-resistant design. Dr. Yadav also has industrial experience in the structural design of buildings in Africa, Middle East, and India regions. He has been a part of more than twenty projects as a structural engineer and peer reviewer.

briefinfo: Dr. Divyesh Varade is a budding expert on remote sensing and spatial analysis. Dr. Varade is working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics Engineering at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, since 2020. He received his PhD degree in Civil Engineering with specialization in Geoinformatics from IIT Kanpur in 2020 and his master’s degree in the International Program on Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology at the Technical University of Munich. He received the IEEE Senior Member title in November 2023, received the runner-up award for best PhD thesis by IEEE GRSS India in 2020. He has served several reputed journals as a referee with more than 100 listed reviews, and is also serving as the review editor for Frontiers in Remote Sensing for the microwave remote sensing section. He is also serving as the editorial board member for the journal All Earth. His primary research interests are focused on deepening the understanding of the cryosphere using remote sensing, agricultural applications of microwave remote sensing, and inversion of surface geophysical parameters. His expertise on remote sensing (multispectral, hyperspectral, thermal, microwave) integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis and machine learning. His research interests further include studies on glaciers, sea ice, and permafrost. His research also includes deriving remote sensing-based solutions to broader problems in Earth observation, agriculture, and water resources.

professionalbackground:
Dr. Divyesh M. Varade is an expert on remote sensing encompassing the domains of hyperspectral, thermal, and microwave remote sensing. Dr. Varade is presently working as the Assistant Professor of Geomatics at the Department of Civil Engineering, Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. He is a research collaborator at the ATAL Centre for Artificial Intelligence at MJPRU-Bareilly and an advisor/consultant to Geo Climate Risk Solutions Pvt. Ltd. He received his Ph.D. degree in Geoinformatics from the Department of Civil Engineering, IIT Kanpur in 2020. He was the IEEE GRSS India runner-up awardee for the best Ph.D. thesis category in 2020. In his doctoral research, he devised novel methods for the qualitative and quantitative characterization of snow using multi-sensor remote sensing data. His research integrates physical and mathematical modelling, information theory, statistical analysis, and machine learning. He is also avidly interested in the hydrological and environmental problems related to the high mountain regions and polar sea ice. Prior to his Ph.D. Dr. Varade pursued his graduate degree in the International Earth Oriented Space Science and Technology (ESPACE) Program with a specialization in remote sensing at the Technical University of Munich. He conducted his graduate research on change of detection of buildings at the Division of Photogrammetry and Image Analysis, Remote Sensing Technology Institute, German Aerospace Centre. Dr. Varade holds an undergraduate degree in Electronics and Communication Engineering, which significantly influenced his interests in radar remote sensing.

Dr. Varade has published well-received articles on snow geophysics and snowpack characterization. He is a referee for several highly reputed hydrology/water resource and remote sensing journals. He is also a member of several international associations on remote sensing, hydrology/water resources, and cryosphere. His research interests are developing novel methods using remote sensing for problems broadly related to the cryosphere, hydrology, water resources, and environmental issues. Dr. Varade is also avidly interested in applying AI/ML techniques for qualitative and quantitative problems in Earth observation. He has reviewed several manuscripts on applied ML with remote sensing in sea ice and snow studies and is presently working on the application of deep learning techniques for the identification of avalanche debris, snow depth mapping, and various other problems in Cryosphere.

briefinfo:
Dr. Dnyaneshwar Bhawangirkar is presently serving as an Assistant Professor in the department of chemical engineering at IIT Jammu in India. 

His research interest is in ‘Gas Hydrates or Clathrate Hydrates’ focusing on various applications, including methane recovery from the natural gas hydrates present beneath the seafloor, carbon sequestration in geological marine sedimentary locations, hydrogen and natural gas storage, and flow assurance through both modelling and experimental approaches. He is also exploring different strategies for improving the oil recovery via novel EOR technologies.

Kindly email at dnyaneshwar.bhawangirkar@iitjammu.ac.in for any possible collaborations.
researchinterest:
Gas hydrates or clathrate hydrates for their applications in NGH development, CCS, energy storage and flow assurance (Modelling and Experiments)

  1. Methane recovery from natural gas hydrates (NGH development)
  2. Carbon capture and sequestration in porous medium as hydrates (CCS)
  3. Natural gas and hydrogen storage as hydrates (Energy storage)
  4. Hydrate inhibition in oil and gas pipelines for flow assurance

Additionally, we are also equally interested in developing strategies for enhancing the oil recovery (EOR) through different approaches.

2. We are also looking forward for industry partners for any possible collaborative projects related to NGH developement, carbon sequestration, hydrogen storage, flow assurance and enhanced oil recovery related topics.

research_experience:
1. Research Scholar, 2019-2023, University of New Mexico, USA

Area of work: Induced changes in the brain via affective imagery, decision-making dynamics, reward positivity, reward learning, and attentional bias in clinical populations.
 

2. Research Assistant, 2017-2019, Idaho State University, USA

Area of work: Sexual impulsivity and monetary delay discounting, Metacognition health and memory (perceptive memory, procedural memory, semantic memory).
researchinterest: Neural mechanisms of reward processing, decision-making and aberrant behaviours in clinical versus healthy populations through neuroimaging methods, delay discounting, metacognition, and memory dynamics.
academicinterests:
Dr. Garima Singh’s academic interests lie at the intersection of cognitive neuroscience and psychology, with a particular focus on the neural mechanisms underlying reward processing, decision-making, and aberrant behaviors in clinical versus healthy populations. Using advanced neuroimaging methods, including EEG, her work aims to deepen our understanding of how neural pathways impact cognitive and behavioral health, especially in relation to psychiatric conditions affecting the reward system.

In addition to these areas, Dr. Singh pursues separate research in delay discounting, metacognition, and memory dynamics, exploring how these processes influence decision-making and cognitive function across various contexts. Her broader research includes examining the role of stress, depression, and substance misuse on brain function, and investigating social factors such as memory distortion from social media and social influences on memory recall.

Dr. Singh’s research ultimately aims to develop effective diagnostic tools and contribute to a comprehensive understanding of cognitive health, advancing interventions for mental health challenges and aberrant behaviors.

academicinterests:
As an academic most of our time is dedicated towards students learning. Classroom engagement is an important aspect of teaching. I work on development of new techniques of class room teaching. This is based on the epistemology represented in Indian thought and the mythological construct of the subcontinent. Within this theme we are focusing on many different aspect of Engineering Education

  1. Active classroom engagement

  2. Development of indigenous pedagogy techniques for engineering education

  3. Active laboratory engagement and assessment

  4. Innovations in summative assessment

briefinfo: An enthusiastic and self-motivated researcher and academic. My vision is "Innovation with sustainability for society". Me and my research group look towards energy effective, bio-inspired, biochemical processes for solving current socitial problems. This is done by involving chemical engineering and biological science principles for process development using energy efficient intensification techniques.

briefinfo: Dr. R. Gurubrahamam received M. Sc. and Ph. D. (2013) from the School of Chemistry, University of Hyderabad under the supervision of Prof. M. Periasamy. He pursued postdoctoral studies in the field of asymmetric organocatalysis at Prof. K. Chen's research group, NTNU, Taipei. Then he moved to Prof. B. Olofsson's research group to work on the hypervalent iodine(III) reagents as a Wenner Gren postdoctoral fellow at the Dept. of Organic Chemistry, Stockholm University, Stockholm, Sweden. He spent a short time at Syngene International Ltd., Bangalore in 2019. He has been working as an assistant professor at IIT Jammu in the field of asymmetric synthesis since October 2019.

briefinfo: I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mathematics, Indian Institute of Technology, Jammu. I have finished my Ph.D in IIT Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sivaramakrishnan Sivasubramanian. Subsequently, I was a Post-doc in HRI Prayagraj, IISc Bangalore, and IISER Kolkata.

Areas of Research:
i. Development of hybrid multilevel power converter topologies for FACTS and HVDC applications.
ii. Power quality improvement of three phase AC grids, including reactive power compensation, unbalance mitigation, and harmonic compensation.
iii. Control algorithm development for capacitor voltage regulation in multilevel converters under unbalanced three-phase conditions.
iv. Application of multilevel converters for simultaneous charging of multiple electric vehicles (EVs).

IgrenEnergi Services Private Limited

Area of Work: Development of Battery Management Systems (BMS) for two- and four-wheeler electric vehicles, including passive and active cell balancing of battery packs
briefinfo: Ibhan Chand Rath received the B.Tech. degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from the International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar, India, in 2014, and the Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from IIT Bombay, India, in 2024. He worked as a System Integration and Testing Consultant at Igrenenergi Services Private Limited, Research Park, IIT BOMBAY, from May 2024 to December 2025. Currently, he is serving as Assistant Professor in the Electrical Engineering Department of IIT JAMMU from January 2026.
researchinterest: Grid connected hybrid multivelvel converter topology design, Flexible AC Transmission Systems (FACTs), High Voltage Direct Current (HVDC), Grid integration of Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), Battery Management System (BMS), V2G and G2V EV charging applications,
academicinterests: Power Electronics, power systems, electric machines, and control systems

academicinterests:
At the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu, my academic contributions span both undergraduate and postgraduate levels, with a strong focus on the chemistry of nanomaterials, functional porous frameworks, coordination chemistry, and catalysis. I am actively involved in curriculum development and the delivery of foundational and advanced courses in chemistry, aiming to bridge fundamental science with real-world applications.

Courses Taught

  1. Introduction to Fundamental and Applied Chemistry (SCL001U2M)
    Offered since 2020 – Undergraduate core course introducing key concepts in physical, inorganic, and organic chemistry.

  2. Chemistry Laboratory (SCP001U1M)
    Offered since 2020 – Practical training in basic experimental techniques and safety protocols.

  3. Chemistry of Nanostructured Materials (CYL734)
    Offered since 2020 – Postgraduate elective covering synthesis, properties, and applications of nanomaterials.

  4. Functional Nano Porous Materials towards Sustainable Applications (CYL007P6E)
    Course Coordinator (Since 2021) – Interdisciplinary course exploring the design of porous materials for energy, environment, and catalysis.

  5. Advanced Coordination Chemistry (CYL010P1M)
    Offered in 2022 and 2023 – Focused on the bonding, structure, and reactivity of coordination compounds.

  6. Bioinorganic and Bio-inspired Transition Metal Catalysis (CYL031P2E)
    Offered since 2023 – Investigates transition metal systems in biological and catalytic contexts.

  7. Preparatory Chemistry I & II (CYL031P2E / CY-0-06(TO))
    Foundation-level courses designed to strengthen students’ background in chemistry before advanced study.

  8. Instrumental Methods of Analysis (CYL019P2M)
    Covers analytical techniques such as spectroscopy, chromatography, and electrochemical methods relevant to research and industry.

research_experience:
Our group’s research primarily focuses on the design and development of new materials and their hybrids to address current challenges associated with the energy crisis and environmental issues. The objective of this perspective is to delve into recent developments in emerging hybrid two-dimensional (2D) porous materials (H2DPMs), utilizing various 2D materials in conjunction with nanoporous materials through primary or secondary bonding interactions. We aim to highlight recent advances in synthesis strategies, characterization techniques, and the progress made in energy storage/conversion as well as environmental and industrial applications.

briefinfo:
Kolleboyina Jayaramulu (Ram) is an Associate Professor in the Department of Chemistry and Dean International Relations at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu) and a Visiting Professor at the Centre for Energy and Environmental Technologies, VSB -Technical University of Ostrava, Czech Republic here at IIT Jammu he served as Head Of Department until 2022. His extensive research expertise lies in hybrid porous materials, 2D materials, and their hybrids, focusing on diverse applications such as electrocatalysis, and environmental concerns (water purification, gas storage/separation, harvesting).

Prior to his role at IIT Jammu, Ram served as a senior researcher at the Regional Centre of Advanced Technologies and Materials in the Czech Republic and the Technical University Munich in Germany. He is a recipient of the Alexander von Humboldt Postdoctoral Fellowship in Germany and an ICMS Postdoctoral Fellowship. Ram’s impactful contributions in the field of hybrid porous materials are reflected in his publications in renowned international journals, including Advanced Materials, Angewandte Chemie, Advanced Functional Materials, Nature Communications, and recognition in Advanced Engineering News and Advanced Science News. His research has been featured in various cover arts, showcasing the significance of his work in the scientific community.

jointresearchwork:
In addition to foundational materials development, our research group is deeply committed to interdisciplinary collaboration and product-oriented innovation. We actively pursue translational research by integrating advanced materials into practical systems with broad societal impact. Key collaborative efforts span across multiple engineering disciplines, including:

  • Mechanical Engineering – for the development of flexible, stretchable, and mechanically robust devices.

  • Materials Engineering – for composite design, structural integration, and materials optimization.

  • Civil Engineering – for designing geopolymer concrete systems embedded with nanomaterials for self-healing, pollutant capture, and enhanced durability.

  • Electrical Engineering – for designing functional sensors and incorporating advanced materials into IoT-based platforms and energy-harvesting systems.

  • Environmental Engineering – for atmospheric water harvesting using functional porous frameworks and membrane-based systems.

  • Chemical Engineering – for electro-photo-thermal CO₂ reduction by integrating hybrid catalysts into scalable, sustainable energy platforms.

Our research is inherently translational, targeting industry-relevant solutions through the convergence of materials science, engineering design, and device prototyping. This interdisciplinary strategy enables the practical deployment of next-generation materials, bridging the gap between academic discovery and real-world technology.

teachingengagements:
Actively engaged in teaching a diverse portfolio of undergraduate and postgraduate courses that align with my research expertise in nanomaterials, coordination chemistry, catalysis, and functional porous materials. My teaching philosophy is rooted in integrating theoretical knowledge with practical application, providing students with a holistic and research-oriented learning experience.

Undergraduate Courses

  • SCL001U2M – Introduction to Fundamental and Applied Chemistry (2020–present)
    Core course introducing foundational principles across physical, organic, and inorganic chemistry.

  • SCP001U1M – Chemistry Laboratory (2020–present)
    Hands-on laboratory training focused on essential experimental techniques, safety, and chemical analysis.

  • CYL031P2E – Preparatory Chemistry I (2023–present)
    Foundational course aimed at reinforcing basic chemistry concepts for incoming students.

  • CY-0-06(TO) – Preparatory Chemistry II (2023–present)
    Continuation of Preparatory Chemistry I, enhancing conceptual clarity and problem-solving skills.

Postgraduate Courses

  • CYL734 – Chemistry of Nanostructured Materials (2020–present)
    Advanced-level course exploring the synthesis, structure, and functional applications of nanomaterials.

  • CYL007P6E – Functional Nano Porous Materials Towards Sustainable Applications (Course Coordinator, 2021–present)
    Focused on the design and use of porous materials in energy storage, catalysis, and environmental remediation.

  • CYL010P1M – Advanced Coordination Chemistry (2022, 2023)
    Covers the structure, bonding theories, and reactivity of coordination complexes with modern perspectives.

  • CYL031P2E – Bio-inorganic and Bio-inspired Transition Metal Catalysis (2023–present)
    Examines catalytic systems inspired by nature and their mechanistic pathways involving transition metals.

  • CYL019P2M – Instrumental Methods of Analysis (Year Offered)
    Comprehensive course on modern analytical instrumentation, including spectroscopy, chromatography, and surface techniques.

Curriculum Development and Programme Leadership

During my tenure as Head of the Department of Chemistry at IIT Jammu (2020–2022), I had the privilege of initiating and leading the establishment of the M.Sc. and MS(R) programmes in Chemistry.

briefinfo: Dr. Jigmi Basumatary has been an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since February 2026. His research focuses on the development and advancement of diverse optical imaging and manipulation tools for studying biological systems.
researchinterest: Dr. Jigmi’s research interests include key technologies such as single-molecule localization microscopy (SMLM), light-sheet microscopy, optical tweezers, and photoacoustic/ultrasound imaging. He adopts a multidisciplinary approach that integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines, along with rigorous qualitative and quantitative analysis. The overarching goal of his research is to develop and extend imaging techniques to address fundamental biological questions.

briefinfo: My research focuses on computational material modelling and design for energy, environmental, pharmaceutical, and personal-care applications.
researchinterest:
Multi-scale Modeling and Simulations: Molecular Simulation, Mathematical Modeling, MD Simulation, DFT, QSAR, Machine Learning

Membrane Development for Energy and Environmental Applications: Solvent Recovery, Water Purification, Hydrogen Economy, Carbon Capture

Advance Nanoporous Materials: Covalent Organic Frameworks (COFs), Metal Organic Frameworks (MOFs), Polymer, Graphene/Graphene oxide , Ionic Liquids

Solid and Liquid Formulation for Personal-care & Pharma: Understanding of Crystallization and Formulation, Transport of Preservative, Drug delivery, Skin Permeation

research_experience: Prof. Gaur has successfully completed a number of funded research projects from various funding agencies like MEiTY, DST, UKIERI, CEFIPRA. He has extensive international collaborations with Universities in USA, UK, Spain, Germany, France, Italy, Russia, Australia, New Zealand and Japan. He has been instrumental in establishing Information Security Group and also initiating M.Tech programs, one in Information Security, in MNIT Jaipur. He has also handled a number of Institutional projects. He has supervised seventeen PhDs till date and currently, ten research scholars, under-graduates and post-graduates are working actively in his research group. Prof. Gaur has published extensively in various peer reviewed journals and conferences and is part of a number of conferences of his research domain.

researchprojects:
Fund Raising/sponsored projects-

Development Finance/Credit for Development

1. Public Enterprise Reform Program Examines state finances to public enterprises and argues for states' disinvestment from the consumer goods sector.

2. Rural Infrastructure Development: Role of RIDF Examines balance disbursement of infrastructure development fund.

3. Assets as Liability? Non Performing Assets in the Indian Commercial Banks- Examines the problem of non performing loans in the Indian banking sector, compares India's NPA with other Asian countries.

4. New Dimensions of Rural Indebtedness in India: An Assessment of the Challenges to the Credit Institutions.

5. Farm Sector in Karnataka: Indebtedness and Risk Management- Examines farmers' access to formal credit.

6. Implementation of Revival Package for Short-Term Cooperative Credit Societies in the State of Andhra Pradesh- Cooperative credit societies are grass root level financial institutions that make credit accessible to the poor, but many such societies have weak financial health and high level of non-performing loans. This study is about how to reform this sector to make them sustainable using the revival strategy suggested by Dr. Vaidyanathan Committee.

7. Implementation of Revival Package for Short-Term Cooperative Credit Societies in the State of Madhya Pradesh.

8. Implementation of Revival Package for Short-Term Cooperative Credit Societies in  the state of Bihar.

9. Financial Exclusion in Urban Region Takes up small trader class in urban regions and examines the reason behind their financial exclusion in spite of having a banking facility at their vicinity. This study prescribes introduction of kiosk based banking correspondents at the market places.

10. Interest Subvention in Short Term Agricultural Credit: Does it Serve the Purpose- Examines the subsidised credit scheme for farmers. Arrives at policy prescription for the central bank.

11. SHG-Bank Linkage Program in Karnataka: A Critical Evaluation of the Quality and Sustainability of SHGs- Examines (women's) self help group and their bank linkage programme. Looks at subsequent improvement in income and social status and there by empowerment.

12. Peri-urban Regions of Bangalore: Changing Structure of Economic, Social, and Financial Paradigms.

13. Financial Flows in the Rural-Urban Interface of Bangalore: Access to Credit- Examines financial infrastructure and access to credit for the peri-urban self employed. 

14. Health Insurance for the Poor and Elderly: is RSBY the Answer? Examines a health insurance scheme .

15. Crop Insurance in Karnataka- Examines various problems with the newly introduced crop insurance scheme in India. Compares with other Asian countries such as China.

16. Financial Inclusion And Gender: A Study Of Selected Developing Nations.

Agriculture and Rural Sector

17. Mapping Farm and Non-farm Linkages in Rural India.

18. The Rural-Urban Interface of Bangalore: A Space of Transition in Agriculture, Economics, and Society.

19. Women in the online marketplace: Are rural women offline in India?

Industry and Labour

20. Food Distribution in Emerging Markets: The Case of Indian Seafood.

21. Contractual Employment in Selected Manufacturing Enterprises in Karnataka.

22. India's Export of Selected BPO Services.

23. Pandemic and the Credit Based Stimulus Package for the MSME Sector: A Study of Selected Peri-Urban Regions of Bengaluru (Ongoing).

24. Livelihood and Gender in the Urban Space (Ongoing).

25. Evaluation of the Samruddhi Scheme: Employment Generation Scheme (Ongoing).

26. Importance of MSMEs in India and Japan (Ongoing).

Theory and Methodology

27. Methodologies for Estimating District Income in the Indian States.

28. Theorizing Farm Nonfarm Linkages in Rural India.

academicinterests:
Research Areas
Area: G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) biology and Cell signaling
Approaches: Biochemical, biophysical and chemogenetic based methods.

Research Interests
G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) act as membrane gatekeepers and are the pleiotropic mediators of cellular signaling. GPCRs are the largest receptor superfamily in the human genome, and it plays indispensable roles in almost all the known physiological processes. As a result, almost half of the currently prescribed drugs work by targeting GPCRs. Our lab’s long-term focus is to understand the underlying molecular basis of GPCR functioning in many physiological and pathophysiological processes. We employ multiple orthogonal and combinatorial approaches ranging from biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches in our studies. Leveraging this molecular information will enable us to design better and safer GPCR therapeutic interventions.

Current research themes:

     1. Molecular basis of interaction and modulation by the pore-forming toxins on GPCRs.

     2. Molecular function of arrestin fold proteins in protozoan parasites.

     3. Mechanism of β-arrestin bias in G protein-coupled receptors for designing

         next-generation drugs with reduced side effects.

For details, please visit the lab webpage: https://sites.google.com/view/receptorbiologylaboratory

briefinfo:
Our research focuses on membrane proteins, particularly G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs). Our primary goal is to unravel the molecular basis of GPCR function and leverage this knowledge to develop improved medicines for the future. Our research aims to enhance our understanding of membrane proteins and their role in cellular communication, paving the way for targeted and effective therapeutic interventions.

researchinterest:
The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics.

To achieve this, the lab employs a range of research techniques, including biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods, we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways.

The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly, to investigate the molecular basis of interaction and modulation between GPCRs and pore-forming toxins. The insight into toxin influence on GPCRs can yield underappreciated signaling responses and immunomodulatory effects on our physiology. A holistic understanding of the dynamics of toxin-receptor interaction and signaling will open up new opportunities to develop novel therapeutic interventions.

Secondly, the lab is dedicated to unraveling the mechanisms behind signaling bias in GPCRs. GPCR biasing involves selectively activating or inhibiting specific signaling pathways downstream of a GPCR while sparing others. GPCR biasing holds great promise for developing next-generation therapeutics with enhanced efficacy and reduced side effects. Our continuing research effort in this direction is expected to uncover new insights into GPCR signaling and facilitate the design of novel drugs that can selectively modulate specific signaling pathways, leading to more precise and personalized treatments for various diseases.

research_experience: Dr. Bhattacharjee has worked as a Research Scientist at the LEAP Lab, Indian Institute of Science Bangalore from June 2024 to November 2024. His work focussed on developing Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) systems for multi-lingual, multi-talker, code-mixing and code-switching conversations in Indian languages. Prior to that, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at the IDIAP Research Institute in Switzerland from May 2023 to May 2024. He worked on developing ASR solutions for Air Traffic Control communications with particular focus on improving recognition of rare domain specific words. He was also engaged as a Senior Project Engineer at IIT Dharwad in a MeitY funded project under the National Language Translation Mission of the Govt. of India. The project goal was to build speaker recognition systems for Indian languages. Dr. Bhattacharjee has also worked as an Assistant Project Engineer in a DST, Govt. of India sponsored project at IIT Guwahati that aimed at developing online handwriting recognition systems for the Assamese language. Dr. Bhattacharjee completed his Ph.D. in Signal Processing & Machine Learning from IIT Guwahati, under the supervision of Prof. S. R. Mahadeva Prasanna and Dr. Prithwijit Guha. He completed his M.Tech. in Computer Technology from NIT Raipur abd B.Tech. in Information Technology from Assam (Central) University Silchar. My research interests lie in the areas of speech signal processing, machine/deep learning, audio classification/detection, speaker verification, dialect/language identification and vocal mode classification.

briefinfo:
Welcome to "Thermal and Natural Phenomenon Lab (TNPL)"

Navneet Kumar is an Associate Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu. He received his Bachelor (B.Tech) & Masters (M.Tech) degrees from IIT (BHU) Varanasi in 2010 and Doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2017 all in Mechanical Engineering.

His research interests lie in understanding the Natural processes and phenomena including fluid & energy flows and applying some of these to the existing and advent of cutting edge technological systems. He is further interested in developing suitable sensors (IoT based) for better and timely assessment across various disciplines inluding medical. Some of his personal interests include understanding air pollution dynamics (and devise mitigation techniques), understanding contamination of soil / aquifers / underground water (and decontaminate up to some extent).

His research group explores various fundamental areas including the effect of phase changes (evaporation, transpiration, and evapotranspiration) in various systems like agriculture, surface water dynamics, and electronic cooling devices.

KEY LAB (and connected) FACILITIES:

Time-resolved stereo PIV

PDPA (Phase Doppler Particle Analyzer)

OLYMPUS Tilt Microscope (9000x magnification; -90 to +90 tilt; 3-D tomography)

Contact Angle Meter (min. 1 microL drop)

Infrared Camera (1280x960 pixels, with Telephoto Lens)

Low, medium, and High Power Lasers (100mW to 6W)

Optical Cameras

Constant Temperature Bath (Convective type, 0.01 deg C)

Ultra Precision Weighing Scales

briefinfo: Dr. Nitesh Malan has been an Assistant Professor in the Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu since April 2026. His research focuses on neurophysiology, computational neuroscience, and biomedical signal processing, with an emphasis on understanding neural mechanisms underlying human behavior and neurological disorders. His work integrates EEG, MEG, and brain–computer interface approaches to develop biomarkers and improve rehabilitation strategies.
researchinterest: Computational neuroscience, neurophysiology and neural oscillations, biomedical signal processing (EEG/MEG), and neurological disorders and rehabilitation, Brain computer Interface.

briefinfo:
Dr. Prasun Halder has been serving the Civil Engineering Department of IIT Jammu as an Assistant Professor since March 2022. Before joining IIT Jammu, he was a Research Fellow at IIT Delhi. Dr. Halder holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical Engineering from IIT Delhi.

His basic research interest lies in experimental and computational geomechanics with focus on foundation engineering, dam-foundation system on jointed rock, ground improvement, and soil dynamics. Currently, his research group at IIT Jammu is working on applied geomechanics domain to better understand the transportation geotechnics in hilly regions, application of geosynthetic-based solution for rock-fall hazard, tunnel & underground space, and cold-weather geotechnics. 

Dr. Halder publishes extensively in journals and conferences of international repute and has successfully secured multiple research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF), Ministry of Textiles (NTTM), and MoRTH. He has received several awards and recognitions from reputed research agencies. He is involved in various professional societies, including the Indian Geotechnical Society and the International Society of Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. ​

BOOK:

"Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" (Eds. Satinder Kaur Brar, Pratik Kumar, Agnieszka Cuprys). Elsevier Publications, ISBN: 0323854222, 9780323854221

About the Book: This book titled "Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" explores the most up-to-date studies in the field of treating water. Through three contributed sections, this comprehensive resource presents the new approach of tackling the demand for a high quality, economic and green water treatment system. This modular strategy allows for a customized retrofit solution to constantly changing parameters that are dependent on current demand and requirements. The book summarizes the principles of modular design, as well as current developments and perspectives. Chapters include global case studies, flow diagrams and charts to provide readers with the tools they need to implement these techniques in their own work. As such, this book is a must have for researchers, students and professors working in water and wastewater.

bookchapter:
BOOK:

"Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" (Eds. Satinder Kaur Brar, Pratik Kumar, Agnieszka Cuprys). Elsevier Publications, ISBN: 0323854222, 9780323854221

About the Book: This book titled "Modular Treatment Approach for Drinking Water and Wastewater" explores the most up-to-date studies in the field of treating water. Through three contributed sections, this comprehensive resource presents the new approach of tackling the demand for a high quality, economic and green water treatment system. This modular strategy allows for a customized retrofit solution to constantly changing parameters that are dependent on current demand and requirements. The book summarizes the principles of modular design, as well as current developments and perspectives. Chapters include global case studies, flow diagrams and charts to provide readers with the tools they need to implement these techniques in their own work. As such, this book is a must have for researchers, students and professors working in water and wastewater.

briefinfo: Priyanka Mishra completed her Bachelor’s in Electrical Engineering from Galgotia’s College of Engineering and Technology, India in 2013. She received her M. Tech and Ph. D. degrees in Energy Science and Engineering from Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India in 2017 and 2022, respectively. Her Masters’ work won as best post award by Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India. Her Ph. D. work is awarded by POSOCO, India in doctoral category as one of the best Ph. D. Thesis in Power Systems. She worked as a Postdoctoral Associate at State University of New York at Stony Brook, New York, USA from April, 2022 to October, 2023. Key milestones during this research were development of provably resilient, programmable networked microgrids. She worked as HVDC Control and Protection Engineer at GE Renewables, Noida, India from November 2023 to January 2026. Out of her research, she published 7 journals and 5 conference papers, including one book chapter. She received outstanding reviewer award in IEEE Journal of Ocean Engineering in 2022. Her research interests include solar photovoltaic power generation and power system protection integrating inverter-based renewable energy sources.
researchinterest: Protection of microgrid integrating renewable energy sources (solar power, wind power, etc); Neural active fault management control of power-electronic converter-based renewable energy sources; Time-domain relaying; Converter transformer protection.
academicinterests: Power Systems; Switchgear and protection devices; Power System Operation and Control

briefinfo: I am Dr. Priyatosh Jena, currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. I received my Ph.D. degree in Electrical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology (Banaras Hindu University), Varanasi, India, my M.Tech. degree in Electrical Engineering with specialization in Renewable Energy and Energy Management from the National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh, India, and my B.Tech. degree in Electrical and Electronics Engineering from the International Institute of Information Technology, Bhubaneswar, India. I am a member of the IEEE Power Electronics Society (PELS) Educational Videos Committee. I was awarded the Chairman’s Gold Medal and the Institute Gold Medal from NIT Arunachal Pradesh in recognition of outstanding academic performance in the M.Tech program. I am also a recipient of the 14th GRID-INDIA Power System Awards (GIPSA) 2025–26.
researchinterest: Battery Charging System, Bidirectional Converter, Reconfigurable Power Converter, and Soft-Switched Converter.
currentopenings: I have openings for PhD students in the area of Power Electronics and Energy Conversion Systems, including AC-DC/DC-DC converters and related topics. Candidates with a good background in power electronics, control systems, and circuit design are encouraged to apply. Interested students may contact me for more details.
academicinterests: Digital Control of Power Converters, Electric Vehicle Technology and Charging Systems, Network Theory, Power Converter Design, Power Quality and FACTS Devices, Renewable Energy Systems, and Switch Mode Power Supplies.

awardsandhonours:
A1. GRID-INDIA Power System Award (GIPSA) – Ph.D. Category (2025–26)
Awarded to Priyatosh Jena for outstanding doctoral research titled “Soft-Switching Modulation Techniques for Bidirectional Dual Active Bridge Converter Based Battery Charging Systems.” The award is conferred under the CSR initiative of Grid Controller of India Limited (GRID-INDIA), formerly Power System Operation Corporation Limited (POSOCO), recognizing exemplary Ph.D. research contributions in power systems and allied domains.

A2. Best Thesis Award – IEEE STPEC 2025, Goa
Priyatosh Jena received the Best Ph.D. Thesis Award at the 2025 4th IEEE International Conference on Smart Technologies for Power, Energy and Control (STPEC) 2025 held in Goa, India, for the doctoral thesis titled “Soft-Switching Modulation Techniques for Bidirectional Dual Active Bridge Converter Based Battery Charging Systems.”

A3. Chairman’s Gold Medal (2018)
Awarded to Priyatosh Jena for securing the highest CGPA among all students of the Electrical Engineering branch in the postgraduate program (2016–2018) at National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh. The medal was presented during the 5th Convocation by the Chief Guest Sanjay Hemant Inamdar.

A4. Institute Gold Medal (2018)
Awarded to Priyatosh Jena for achieving the highest CGPA in the Renewable Energy and Energy Management M.Tech. program offered by the Department of Electrical Engineering at National Institute of Technology Arunachal Pradesh. The medal was presented during the 5th Convocation by Anil Dattatraya Sahasrabudhe, Chairman of All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE).

briefinfo:
Hello! I am Rahul Dattatraya Kitture. 

I completed Ph.D. from Savitribai Phule Pune University, Pune under the supervision of Prof. Ravindra Shripad Kulkarni and Prof. S. A. Katre in 2014.  

I was a Post-Doctoral Fellow in HRI, Allahabad (2015-2017). 

I was a Post-Doctoral Fellow with SERB-NPDF in IISER Mohali (2017-2019).  

Currently I am Assistant Professor in Mathematics at IIT Jammu (from September 18, 2020).
researchinterest: My current area of research is in Group Theory and Representation Theory.
researchsupervised: Currently I have one Ph.D scholar - Miss. Sonakshee Arora (SRF) - under supervision, who is working in Character Theory of Finite Groups.

briefinfo: Rajkumar Velu, PhD, is an Assistant Professor, Department of Mechanical Engineering and Dual Affilation: Department of Materials Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu and worked as Visiting Assistant Professor at NTUST Taiwan in 2024. Priorly worked as a visiting research associate (2023) and Research Fellow at the Centre for Laser Aided Intelligent Manufacturing (CLAIM), University of Michigan, USA, and as a Post-Doctoral Research Fellow at the Digital Manufacturing and Design Centre (DManD), Singapore University of Technology and Design, Singapore. He completed his PhD thesis at the Auckland University of Technology, New Zealand, in selective laser sintering of specific biopolymer composites for biomedical application. Dr Velu is also the recipient of the prestigious GATE scholarship, best all-rounder award, best outgoing student gold medal for his academic performances at the university level. Consequently, he has considerable insights and knowledge on different polymer, hydrogels and composite materials used explicitly for the 3D printing process. He also has substantial experience in material and component characterization targeting specific responses, evaluating the complex thermo-mechanical aspects of powder bed processing, extrusion processing, and post-mortem studies for mechanical, electrical and biological attributes. More than 30 publications were generated from his research activities, and he received a scientific research award at an international conference conducted by Philadelphia University, Amman, Jordan 2010. Also, a recipient of the “young scientist award” 2018 at the Annual Conference on 3D printing and bioprinting in Health care, Singapore. Dr Velu’s research interests encompass engineering design, optimization, and developing advanced manufacturing processes (Additive Manufacturing) and materials for designing and fabrication for fuel cell, biomedical and aerospace applications. Moreover, based on his current research practice, he is involved in developing Artificial Intelligence into an additive manufacturing process to fabricate end-use products and eliminate post-mortem analysis.

currentopenings:
Current Openings

B.Tech./B.E. Internships – Open

Motivated undergraduate students from Mechanical, Materials, Metallurgy, Polymer, or Biomedical Engineering backgrounds with a strong academic record are invited to apply for research internships.
Please email your CV and academic transcripts with a brief note of interest.

M.Tech./M.E. Internships – Open

Postgraduate students interested in additive manufacturing, materials processing, or computational modeling are encouraged to apply.
Send your CV, transcripts, and a short cover letter outlining your research interests.

Ph.D. Research Positions – Open

Applications are welcome from highly driven candidates with a background in:

  • Additive Manufacturing (polymers, metals, composites)

  • Biomedical Implants, Bioprinting

  • Aerospace, Automotive, and Energy Materials

  • Computational Materials Science

Prior experience in experimental techniques or simulation tools (e.g., LAMMPS, FEA) is a plus.

Email your CV (with publications, if any) and a Statement of Purpose.
For application timelines and requirements, refer to the IIT Jammu Ph.D. admissions page.

researchinterest:
Dr. Rajkumar Velu’s research is centered on additive manufacturing (AM) and advanced materials engineering, with a particular emphasis on the development, processing, and application of polymeric, metallic, and composite materials for high-performance and functional components. His multidisciplinary research integrates experimental and computational approaches to explore the following key areas:

  1. Selective laser sintering of reactive polymers

  2. Additive manufacturing of functionally graded structures

  3. Fabrication of multifunctional components using continuous carbon fibre 3D printing

  4. 3D bioprinting for biomedical and energy applications

  5. SLM and Direct energy deposition of super alloys for aerospace applications

briefinfo:
Dr. Riya Bhowmik is an Assistant Professor of Civil Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. ​ She holds a Ph.D. in Geotechnical and Geoenvironmental Engineering from IIT Delhi. Dr. Bhowmik has been with IIT Jammu since October 2020 and has also served as a Visiting Fellow at the University of Salerno, Italy.

Her research focuses on geosynthetics, ground improvement, and disaster mitigation. ​ Dr. Bhowmik publishes extensively in international journals and conferences, and has successfully secured several research projects from funding agencies such as SERB (presently ANRF) and Ministry of Textiles. ​She has been recognized as one of the 75 Influential Indian Women in Geotechnical Engineering and has received several awards, including the SERB National Post-Doctoral Fellowship and the Indian Geotechnical Society-M.D. Desai Memorial YGE Award. ​ Dr. Bhowmik is involved in various professional societies, including the Indian Geotechnical Society and the International Society of Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. ​
currentopenings:
Research Positions for prospective Ph.D Students: Available

Motivated, bright, and sincere candidates with strong academic background in Civil and Geotechnical Engineering, and preferable expertise in Soil Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering, Geosynthetics, and Numerical and Experimental Geotechnics may email their CV (preferably with list of publications) with a cover letter (1 page). The candidates are also requested to visit the Homepage of IIT Jammu for regular updates on admissions to various programs.

researchinterest:
Research interests

  • Protection works against landslides (debris flow/rockfall/mudslides) in hilly regions
  • Use of geosynthetics for hill road infrastructure
  • Rock-shed tunnels under rockfall impacts
  • Geosynthetics-reinforced infrastructure in seasonally frozen regions
  • Tunnelling-induced instability and surface settlements

briefinfo: Samaresh Bera is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Computer Science and Engineering, IIT Jammu. Before that, he was a C.V. Raman Post-Doctoral Fellow in the Department of Electrical Communication Engineering, IISc Bangalore, India. He also worked as a Research Engineer at INRIA Sophia Antipolis in joint collaboration with Nokia Bell Labs, France. He obtained his PhD and MS degrees from the Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, India. He is a Senior Member of the IEEE and a Member of the ACM.

briefinfo:
Thank you for visiting my webpage. I am an Assistant Professor in the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Jammu. My research interests are in the simulation and analysis of the fluid dynamics of heated and unheated turbulent plumes which are an ansatz of clouds and forest-fires. I am also collaborating with my colleagues in the domain of heat-transfer in the materials science domain. My core teaching interest is in the intellectually challenging field of thermo-fluids.

I have received my undergraduate (BE) degree from NMAMIT Mangalore in 2004, graduate degree (ME) from IIEST Shibpur in 2007 and doctoral (PhD) degree from IISc Bangalore in 2014; all of them are in Mechanical Engineering.

Please do drop an email if you are interested in fulfilling your higher education ambitions with me.
currentopenings: I am interested in working with curious and diligent PhD scholars in the research domains listed above. Please email me if you are interested.
editorialservices: I have rendered review services for Physics of Fluids, Physica D and Sadhana.
professionalbackground: I have been an Assistant Professor in the at IIT Jammu since December 2017. Prior to that I was an Assistant Professor at the Mechanical Engineering Department IIITDM Jabalpur for a period of 11 months starting December 2016. I have also completed my post-doctoral research for about three years under the renowned (late) Prof. Roddam Narasimha at JNCASR Bangalore.
researchprojects:

researchsupervised:
1. My first PhD scholar was Jalil ul Rehman Khan. For his PhD thesis which he defended in April 2024, he analysed the turbulent/non-turbulent layer on heated and unheated plumes. I am happy to say that Jalil was a diligent student.
  2. I was a co-supervisor for Abhishek Shrivastava. He defenced his thesis in September 2024. Abhishek was the reason for my forays into the analysis of heat-transfer in additive manufacturing processes.

briefinfo:
Bioinformatics & Computational Biology (BCB) Laboratory focuses on improving female reproductive health by decoding regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.

Key Focus Areas:

  • Reproductive Biology
  • Genomics & Single Cell Biology
  • Multi-omics
  • Bioinformatics
  • Computational Biology
  • Bigdata Analysis
  • Cancer Biology
  • Developmental Biology
  • Epigenetics
  • RNA Biology
  • AI/ML in Healthcare
  • Biomarker Discovery

Visit lab website: https://www.subhashlab.in
researchinterest:
Goal

Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health.

Key Focus Areas:

  • Reproductive Biology
  • Genomics & Single Cell Biology
  • Multi-omics

currentopenings:
Available Positions

We're inviting applications to join our Bioinformatics Team! We’re looking for passionate individuals who are eager to translate genomics into clinical applications. This position offers extensive exposure in analyzing advanced multiomic datasets from cutting-edge genomic technologies, as well as opportunities to engage in research and development and collaborate with scientists across India and internationally.

Minimum Qualifications/Requirements:

Master’s (M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent) or Bachelor’s (B.Tech.) degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Sciences with an interest in Life Sciences research, Computational Biology, or Life Sciences. Candidates should be proficient in R, Python, or similar programming languages.

Who are we looking for?

  • Postdoctoral researchers willing to apply for any of the following fellowships such as DBT-BioCARe, SERB-National Post Doctoral Fellowship, or D. S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellow and any others fellowships. We are happy to assist with preparing project proposals.
  • Students interested in pursuing doctoral research in Bioinformatics and Computational Biology. Candidates must have valid GATE score, CSIR/UGC NET, DST-INSPIRE, or PMRF are encouraged to apply through the IIT Jammu Ph.D. program.
  • Students who are interested in undergraduate/postgraduate internships or projects are welcome to contact.

Location: IIT Jammu

If interested, please contact us at: santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in
academicinterests:
Our goal is to integrate high-dimensional data from genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics to predict how regulatory elements—such as enhancers, promoters, and long noncoding RNAs—govern gene expression in specific tissue contexts. To accomplish this, our lab is focusing on exploring the following research areas.

  1. Identifying tissue-specific regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.
  2. Using Single Cell Multiomic Techniques to Understand Complex Regulation of Female Reproductive Tissues.
  3. Regulome Trained Computational Models to Predict Female Reproductive Health Outcomes.
  4. More Information: https://www.subhashlab.in/Research-Areas

briefinfo:
Sartaj Ul Hasan was born in a remote village in the Shahjahanpur district of Uttar Pradesh, in northern India. He is currently an Associate Professor at IIT Jammu. Prior to joining IIT Jammu, he served as a Scientist at DRDO, Delhi, from February 2007 to January 2018.

From September 2011 to December 2012, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at Carleton University, Ottawa, Canada, where he collaborated with Professor Daniel Panario and Professor Steven Wang. He received his Ph.D. in 2010 from the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay under the supervision of Professor Sudhir R. Ghorpade.

His research interests include various aspects of finite fields and their applications.

For more details, please visit his homepage:
https://sites.google.com/site/sartajulhasan/
currentopenings:
Open Ph.D. Position

I am seeking motivated students for a Ph.D. position in the area of finite fields and their applications. Candidates with a strong background in algebra or number theory, and a strong interest in research, are encouraged to apply. Interested applicants are advised to contact me well in advance of the interviews scheduled by IIT Jammu.
