briefinfo:
Propulsion and Energy Research Lab

Our research group specializes in the dynamic fields of aerospace engineering and propulsion. Our primary mission is to develop next-generation micro gas turbine engine technology for energy production and aerospace applications.  Additionally, a key focus of our research is on the development of reusable liquid rocket engines, with a particular emphasis on utilizing throttleable METHALOX injectors.

Brief Bio

Dr. Shanmugadas, assistant professor within the Department of Mechanical Engineering at IIT Jammu, spearheads the Propulsion and Energy Lab, driving pioneering research and innovation related to liquid rocket engines and aero engines. With a primary focus on novel combustor concepts, his expertise extends across different areas including two-phase flow, experimental combustion, and laser flow diagnostics. Currently, his group is involved in the development of a novel gas turbine combustor capable of utilizing various fuels such as Hydrogen, methane, and SAF.

Dr. Shanmugadas is recognized for his contributions to fuel injection systems with many publications and his group has also filed many patents. He is actively engaged with industry and governmental bodies like ISRO and GE Aerospace and promotes impactful collaborations. He also works closely with the National Centre for Combustion Research and Development (NCCRD) at IIT Madras, focusing on advancing gas turbine combustion research. Additionally, he collaborates with the Chair of Space Propulsion and Mobility at Technical University Munich, Germany, in the area of injection concepts for liquid rocket engines.

Dr. Shanmugadas is a professional member of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) and a lifetime member of the Combustion Institute. He serves as the Associate Dean of Academic Affairs at IIT Jammu, contributing to the institution's academic excellence.

Dr. Shanmugadas is the founder of SAP Aerospace, a pioneering deep-tech startup focused on developing cutting-edge aero engines for Unmanned Combat Aerial Vehicles (UCAVs).
currentopenings:
Two PhD positions are available with our group.

1. Development of low-emission combustor technology for micro gas turbine engines

Our group has developed a novel gas turbine combustor concept that focuses on lean burn combustion to reduce emissions and improve efficiency. The objective of this project is to investigate the operational characteristics and performance of different injection systems based on Lean burn combustion concept. The candidate will conduct experimental investigations using advanced laser flow diagnostic techniques to quantify the flow and combustion processes. The proposed research involves detailed investigations with a  radially staged gas turbine injectors to assess their fuel flexibility and performance characteristics. Interested candidates may contact the principal investigator (PI) for more details.

The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team.

2. Development of reusable liquid rocket engine combustors

The rocket industry is pursuing the development of liquid rocket engines with rapid reusable capability. In this regard, our group has made advancements to develop a novel pintle injector concept for reusable methane-oxygen (METHALOX) liquid rocket engines. The objective of this project is to investigate the spray and combustion characteristics of this concept at subcritical operating conditions. The candidate will conduct experimental investigations using advanced optical and laser diagnostic techniques to quantify the spray and combustion processes. The proposed research involves detailed investigations of the effects of injector geometry, operating pressure, and fuel mixture ratio on the performance and stability of the pintle injector. Interested candidates may contact the PI for more details.

The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team.

Junior Research Fellow

ISRO is developing high-capacity launch platforms for futuristic heavy duty launch vehicles. Our group is working in close collaboration with ISRO to support various activities. The project involves detailed investigations on jet in free supersonic crossflow relevant to launch platforms. Rocket engine exhaust is cooled by injecting water in very high quantities to suppress the acoustic noise levels and to reduce the exhaust gas temperature. Physical models of the atomization processes and experimental databases are currently not available in the case of free supersonic crossflow configurations. The free supersonic gas stream structures and shock trails generated from a M=3 convergent-divergent nozzle will be characterized using focused color schlieren imaging, planar laser-induced fluorescence (TR-PLIF) imaging and Time-resolved particle imaging velocimetry (TR-PIV) experiments. Furthermore, predictive analytical models will be developed to correlate the droplet dispersion and spatial SMD with primary breakup parameters as a function of geometric and flow parameters.

The candidate is expected to have a basic degree in Mechanical or Aerospace Engineering and a fundamental understanding of fluid and thermal systems. The candidate should also have good communication and writing skills and be able to work independently and in a team.

Internship and B.Tech Project opportunities

Undergraduate, and postgraduate students interested in different domains of aerospace engineering are encouraged to apply for short-term engagements and internships. Interested candidates may contact the PI directly through email (shanmugadas.kp@iitjammu.ac.in). 

Visit our startup website also to know more about available positions.

briefinfo: I am currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu). Prior to this, I held faculty positions as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical Engineering at IIT Tirupati and in the Department of Electronics and Communication Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Karnataka (NITK), Surathkal. Before transitioning to academia, I worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher in the LEAP Lab at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore. I earned my Ph.D. in Signal Processing from the Department of Electronics and Electrical Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati (IIT Guwahati). My research interests lie in the areas of speech and audio processing, speaker and language diarization, dialect and language identification, as well as the application of signal processing, machine learning, and deep learning techniques.

briefinfo: Dr. Shirsendu Ghosh did his graduation from Jadavpur University with honors in Chemistry (2009). Then he obtained his master’s degree in Chemistry from IIT Kharagpur (2011). He received his Ph.D. degree from the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science (IACS), Kolkata in 2016. After a Postdoctoral stint at Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel (2016-2021) he joined as a research associate at Cornell University, USA (2021-2022). After that, he served as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry, GITAM Hyderabad for two years (2022-2024). Then he joined as an Assistant Professor in the Dept. of Chemistry with a joint affiliation in the Department of Bioscience and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu on December 2024. His area of research revolves around the development and use of new optical spectroscopy and microscopy techniques to decipher the dynamics of biological systems and live cells. More specifically, he developed total internal reflection fluorescence microscopy-based super-resolution microscopic techniques to investigate the role of plasma membrane topography in cancer biology and immunology (such as T-cell signaling pathways and Mast cell signaling pathways). He is also interested in deciphering the consequences of long-range charge reorganization within a protein on the allosteric signaling of proteins which may lead to the development of a pathway to control allosteric signaling. He employs single-molecule fluorescence microscopy and time-resolved confocal microscopy techniques to decipher the changes in polarity, viscosity, and dynamics of different regions of biomolecules (such as proteins, DNA, lipid vesicles, etc.) and live cells. He is also interested in the development of low-cost protein-coated copper nanoclusters for drug delivery and ion sensing. He is the recipient of the prestigious Ramanujan Fellowship (SERB), DST-inspired faculty fellowship, Koshland Prize (Koshland Foundation, Israel), and CSIR-Research Fellowship.

currentopenings:
PhD Position Available in Single Molecule Biophysics Lab

Join Our Cutting-Edge Research Team!

Are you passionate about pushing the boundaries of science? Do you want to explore the complexities of biological processes at the molecular level? We are looking for a highly motivated PhD student to join our dynamic research group in Single Molecule Biophysics.

Project Overview: In this project, you will use advanced biophysical techniques to study the behavior of individual molecules, providing unprecedented insight into cellular processes. You will explore topics such as membrane biophysics, specifically membrane topography and domains, immunological signaling, protein folding, allosteric signaling in proteins, DNA/RNA interactions with small molecules, and other key biological mechanisms in health and disease.

Research Focus:

Super-resolution optical microscopy

Single-molecule fluorescence microscopy

Single-molecule FRET (Fluorescence Resonance Energy Transfer)

Real-time measurements of biomolecular interactions

Candidate Requirements:

A strong background in physics, chemistry, biology, or related fields

A valid NET or GATE qualification

Previous experience with laboratory techniques (e.g., microscopy, spectroscopy) is a plus but not mandatory

Strong problem-solving skills, curiosity, and an eagerness to learn

Ability to work independently and as part of a collaborative team

Excellent written and verbal communication skills

Why Join Us?

Access to state-of-the-art laboratory facilities and equipment

A vibrant, interdisciplinary research environment with opportunities for collaboration

Supportive mentorship and professional development opportunities

A chance to contribute to pioneering work at the intersection of physics, biology, and technology

If you're ready to take on an exciting challenge and contribute to groundbreaking research, apply today!

How to Apply: Please send your CV, and a cover letter detailing your research interests to shirsendu.ghosh@iitjammu.ac.in.

Postdoctoral researchers willing to apply for any of the following fellowships such as DBT-BioCARe, SERB-National Post Doctoral Fellowship, or D. S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellowships. We are happy to assist with preparing project proposals.

currentopenings:
INTERNSHIPS: I am looking for interns for the coming summers between May to July 2024 (dates flexible). I would prefer students who have intentions of continuing their research as a PhD student with me. The available internship projects are:

  • Parallel and distributed computing of computational genomic pipelines on emebedded cluster.
  • RISC V based System-on-Chip Design for Computational Genomic pipelines. 

I am actively looking for PhD students who would like to work in the following projects:

  • Many-core RISC V based SoC for computational genomic applications. The candidate should be good in coding with fundamental understanding of computer architecture, and digital logic design.
  • ML based Advanced Battery Management systems. The candidate should have Bachelors in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and should, also, have done courses on ML/AI. 
  • Molecular diagnostics for water quality monitoring and anti-microbial resistance surveillance. The candidate should have experience in performing wet-lab experiments required for generating genomic data using sequencing machines. This will be an interdisciplinary project between Department of Computer Science and Biosciences and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu.

Feel free to discuss your background if interested. Please, email me your CV if you are interested using the subject: "PhD Application Inquiry."
researchinterest:
The information on internships and PhD vacancies can be found in the Current Openings section below. 

General Research Interests: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines)

Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions,  we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines

Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS, several key areas can be targeted to achieve longer battery life, efficient charge capacity utilization, and an enhanced user experience: Battery Health Monitoring, Optimized Charge Management, Predictive Maintenance and User-Experience Enhancement.

Tsetlin Machines - We are interested in leveraging Tsetlin Machines (TM) for energy-efficient hardware implementations in edgeAI applications. The growing recognition of TMs' capabilities in classification tasks and the evolution of novel architectures for TM have indeed paved the way for exciting opportunities in machine learning at the edge. The hardware/software co-design approach for application-specific TM implementations at the edge holds significant promise. By aligning hardware and software components to the unique requirements and characteristics of TM, we can potentially achieve optimized performance and energy efficiency in edgeAI applications.

briefinfo:
I am an Assistant Professor in Mathematics department, IIT Jammu.

After finishing my integrated MSc from NISER Bhubaneswar I joined ISI Bangalore as a PhD student under Prof. Manish Kumar. My PhD work was on understanding covers of curves in positive characteristic, leading to my dissertation `On the Inertia Conjecture and its Generalizations' that generalizes and provides numerous evidence for a long standing conjecture posed by Abhyankar. I was a visiting fellow at TIFR Mumbai from September 2020 to July 2022. Subsequently, I joined CMI with NBHM in August 2022, and transferred to ISI Kolkata in August 2023.

briefinfo: Dr. Sri Tapaswi Nori has been serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Materials and Metallurgical Engineering at IIT Jammu since April 2026. His research is centered on understanding irradiation-induced effects and developing advanced alloys for nuclear energy applications. Dr. Nori utilizes a range of characterization techniques, including scanning and transmission electron microscopy, synchrotron X-ray experiments, and nanoindentation, to investigate the microstructural and mechanical behavior of alloys before and after neutron or ion irradiation. He has also led several international research projects, such as ESTEEM3, OFFERR, and Joint Research Centre open call.

briefinfo: Hello, I am Suman. I am a microscopist and experimental materials scientist who basically deals with microstructure-property correlation of materials. My research area focuses on the evolution of novel microstructure through fine tuning the thermodynamic properties and its effects on bulk properties. I extensively use Transmission Electron Microscope (TEM) for microstructural characterization. I use non-equilibrium rapid solidification processing techniques for varying the thermodynamic properties of materials and mesoscale modeling technique (Phase-Field Simulation) for studying the different physical attributes during microstructural evolution and phase transformation. I usually correlate mechanical and transport properties of materials with the evolved microstructure. You can visit the personal web page for more details.

academicinterests:
Research Area:

Partial Differential Equations and Numerics. More precisely, I am interested in hyperbolic conservation laws and numerics, nonlinear dispersive equations, degenerate convection-diffusion equations, discontinuous Galerkin method for evolution equations, optimal control and inverse problems related to evolution equations.

Teaching Interests:

Ordinary and Partial Differential Equations, Numerical Analysis, Real and Functional Analysis.

briefinfo: Dr. Uday Kiran Roopavath has interdisciplinary expertise in biomedical engineering, nanotechnology, and biotechnology. He has research interests in advanced biofabrication techniques such as 3D printing, 3D bioprinting and scaffold free fabrication techniques involving spheroids and organoids. Currently his research focuses on developing functional materials for bone regeneration, fabrication of tissue engineered blood vessels and organ on chip devices for disease modeling applications. He has supervised multiple Master’s and currently supervising 3 Ph.D. students, founded startup (Medoja Bio Private Limited) in MedTech, and has active collaborations with premier international institutes. His contributions span from biomaterials research to translational medical devices.

briefinfo:
Dr. Ved Prakash Ranjan is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at IIT Jammu, specializing in environmental sustainability, circular economy, and waste management. Previously, he was a postdoctoral researcher in the Circular Chemical Engineering division of the Faculty of Science and Engineering at Maastricht University, The Netherlands.

His research focuses on enhancing the recyclability of end-of-life vehicle (ELV) plastics, optimizing pre-treatment processes, and improving feedstock quality to drive sustainable material recovery. He is particularly interested in addressing the identification challenges of black plastics, a growing concern in automotive and waste electrical and electronic equipment (WEEE) plastic recycling industries, and assessing the performance indicators of compounded materials.

His earlier work extensively explored the formation, distribution, and impact of microplastics (MPs) in technical and environmental systems. He is dedicated to developing efficient methodologies for detecting, identifying, and quantifying MPs, aiming to systematically understand their fate, transport, and interactions with organic and chemical pollutants.

Through his research and academic engagement, he strives to contribute to circular economy solutions, sustainable waste management strategies, and innovative recycling approaches that bridge scientific advancements with industry applications.

Keywords: ELV Plastics; Resource recovery; Circular economy; Solid waste management; Plastic waste management; Recyclability assessment; Fate and Transport of Microplastic; Standardization of Microplastic Assessment

briefinfo:
Our research group focuses on nanotechnology applications in bioengineering, with an emphasis on developing innovative technologies at the nanoscale and exploring their biomedical applications. Key areas of research include:

  1. Technology Development at Nanoscale: Portable Diagnostics, Biosensors, Single molecule/Single Cell Techniques etc.
  2. Biomedical Applications of Nanostructures: Bioimaging, AMR, Cancer Therapy, Gene Therapy etc.

With a strong interdisciplinary approach, our lab integrates biotechnology, nanotechnology, electronics, chemistry, and materials science to create healthcare solutions that are both accessible and affordable. Details can be accessed at https://medsenselab.com/
currentopenings:
Students interested in Nanomaterials for biomedical applications, specifically sensors, bioimaging probes, theranostic etc. are welcome to contact us by email for Ph.D. and Postdoctoral positions in the research group. email: vinay.sharma@iitjammu.ac.in 
Candidates willing to explore external postdoc awards are welcome throughout the year under different programs.

briefinfo:
The general theme of our research focuses on answering the fundamental question of how cells deal with DNA damage, particularly DNA double-strand breaks —one of the most critical threats to genomic integrity..

We use a combination of cutting-edge single-molecule DNA curtains technology along with biochemical, genetic, and cell biological approaches to mechanistically understand how DNA repair pathways safeguard genomic stability. A key aspect of our work also explores how these DNA repair mechanisms are finely tuned during gamete formation, ensuring faithful transmission of genetic information across generations.
researchinterest: Single molecule DNA curtains, DNA Repair, Meiosis, Genomic stability, Reproductive disorders
